question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 administratively shuts down GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3↔R4 link,
192.0.0.16/30). The OSPF Area 0 adjacency between R3 and R4 drops. R4's
Loopback0 (192.0.2.4) becomes unreachable from R2 via R3. R4 is now
isolated from the OSPF underlay — its iBGP session to R2 fails because
R2 can't route to 192.0.2.4. Data plane between PE-A and PE-B is
black-holed.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R3, GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3<->R4 link, 192.0.0.16/30) was administratively shut down.
With that link down, the OSPF area-0 adjacency between R3 and R4 can no longer form, so R4's Loopback0 (192.0.2.4) is no longer advertised into the underlay and becomes unreachable through R3; since R3 is the only OSPF transit between R2 and R4, R2 cannot route to 192.0.2.4, the loopback-sourced iBGP session to R4 cannot establish, and PE-A<->PE-B traffic is black-holed.
Fix: no shutdown on R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1.
expected_symptoms_summary:
Interface state change (%PKT_INFRA-LINK-5-CHANGED):
- R3: GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 → Administratively Down.

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 no shutdown

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:12:05.025 UTC: ifmgr[333]: %PKT_INFRA-LINK-5-CHANGED : Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1, changed state to Administratively Down
